methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $125M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Kyoto Fusioneering is a Series C nuclear energy company founded by Satoshi Konishi that has raised $125M+ to date. The broader nuclear energy sector comprises 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 7 private firms including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Last Energy, though comparable same-stage or adjacent-stage peer valuations are not sufficiently available.

Given data constraints, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised ($125M) as a base proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement yields an implied valuation of $212M. This method is the sole quantitatively defensible approach available, as peer-median triangulation cannot be applied due to insufficient narrow-set comparables in the nuclear energy space at similar valuation tiers or stages.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $181M to $244M, centered on $212M. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly comparable transaction data, which limits confidence in the stage-progression multiplier; the nascent and heterogeneous nature of advanced nuclear subsectors; and capital intensity dynamics that may diverge from typical software/biotech progression curves. The valuation should be stress-tested against deployment timelines, regulatory milestones, and sector-specific capital requirements.
